invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Authentication commands (USER and PASS) must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE, MLSD) are issued.
A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be issued before commands that require a data connection (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The RNFR command must be immediately followed by an RNTO command to complete a file renaming operation.
The QUIT command may be issued at any point during the session to terminate the connection.
If the PASS command fails (e.g., response 530), the client must re-issue a USER command before another PASS command.
USER.username: Must be followed by a username string.
PASS.password: Must be followed by a password string.
CWD.path: Must specify a valid path.
PORT.address_port: Must be six comma-separated decimal integers (h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2).
TYPE.type_code: Must be a single character code, typically 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary.
RETR.filename: Must specify the file to be retrieved.
STOR.filename: Must specify the file name for the upload.
RNFR.from_path: Must specify the source file name to be renamed.
RNTO.to_path: Must specify the target file name for the rename.

recommended_actions: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: Standard entry point for FTP authentication.
Standard completion of FTP authentication.
Represents a failed login attempt (530) where the server remains in a state expecting credentials.
Entering passive mode to prepare for data transfer.
Specifying active mode parameters to prepare for data transfer.
While possible if default ports are used, modern FTP usually requires an explicit PASV/PORT command first. Evidence is generic.
Reflects the return to the command state after a transfer completes.

Reinitializes the connection state.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: This is a fundamental requirement of the FTP protocol as defined in RFC 959.

status_changes.description: The RNFR command must be immediately followed by an RNTO command to complete a file renaming operation.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 specifies this exact sequence for renames.

status_changes.description: The QUIT command may be issued at any point during the session to terminate the connection.
status_changes.rationale: QUIT is a global termination command in FTP.

status_changes.description: If the PASS command fails (e.g., response 530), the client must re-issue a USER command before another PASS command.
status_changes.rationale: While often true to reset authentication state, some servers may allow multiple PASS attempts for the same USER before requiring a full reset.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: Must specify a valid path.
status_changes.rationale: The CWD command requires a directory path argument.

status_changes.description: RETR.filename: Must specify the file to be retrieved.
status_changes.rationale: RETR requires a filename argument to locate the resource.

status_changes.description: STOR.filename: Must specify the file name for the upload.
status_changes.rationale: STOR requires a target filename argument.

status_changes.description: RNFR.from_path: Must specify the source file name to be renamed.
status_changes.rationale: RNFR requires the source path.

status_changes.description: RNTO.to_path: Must specify the target file name for the rename.
status_changes.rationale: RNTO requires the destination path.
